tldr: Inference-only pipeline using a frozen DINOv2 encoder with a lightweight custom decoder to generate pixel-wise forgery masks, applying gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding and TTA to produce final submission annotations.
summary: This notebook demonstrates an inference pipeline for scientific image forgery detection using a frozen DINOv2 base encoder paired with a custom lightweight convolutional decoder. It extracts spatial features from the frozen transformer, upsamples them via bilinear interpolation, and passes them through a small Conv2d head to produce per-pixel predictions. The pipeline applies test-time augmentation (horizontal and vertical flips), gradient-based enhancement, and adaptive thresholding to refine masks. A simple area and mean-confidence filter determines the final authentic vs forged label, which is then RLE-encoded for submission.
extensive_summary:
## Model Architecture
The notebook implements a segmentation-style forgery detector. It loads a frozen DINOv2 base encoder (`AutoModel.from_pretrained`) and wraps it in a `DinoSegmenter` class. The encoder's `last_hidden_state` is extracted, the CLS token is discarded, and the remaining tokens are reshaped into a spatial feature map (`B, C, s, s`). A custom `DinoTinyDecoder` head processes these features through a sequence of `Conv2d` layers (768→256→64→1) with ReLU activations, followed by bilinear upsampling to the target resolution (512x512). All encoder parameters are frozen during inference.

## Inference & Augmentation
Input images are resized to 512x512, normalized to [0, 1], and converted to tensors. Test-time augmentation is applied by averaging predictions from the original image, horizontally flipped image, and vertically flipped image. Predictions are passed through a sigmoid function.

## Post-processing & Decision Logic
Raw predictions undergo gradient enhancement using Sobel filters (`alpha_grad=0.35`), followed by Gaussian blurring (3x3 kernel). An adaptive threshold is computed as `mean + 0.3 * std`. The thresholded mask is refined with morphological closing (5x5) and opening (3x3) operations, then resized back to the original image dimensions using nearest-neighbor interpolation. A binary decision is made: if the mask area is less than 400 pixels or the mean prediction inside the mask is below 0.3, the image is labeled authentic; otherwise, it is labeled forged. Forged masks are RLE-encoded for the competition submission.
post_processing: Gradient enhancement via Sobel filters, Gaussian blurring, adaptive thresholding (mean + 0.3*std), morphological closing/opening, area and mean-confidence filtering for binary classification
notable_techniques: Frozen DINOv2 feature extraction with spatial reshaping
Lightweight Conv2d decoder for pixel-wise prediction
Area/mean confidence filtering to suppress false positives
